title: Technical Findings

findings.title: AnyLaw stale dead-route artifact
findings.assessment: The underlying object is no longer present while the route artifact itself remains externally discoverable.
findings.risk_implication: The discovery pathway may persist even though the destination object has failed.
findings.notes: This should be treated as a dead-route artifact rather than a live source page.

findings.title: Brave stale personal-site result

observed_condition.result_behavior: older title or snippet surfaced in search results

findings.assessment: This reflects stale search-surface persistence tied to a removed personal-site asset rather than current live-page availability.
findings.risk_implication: Search users may continue encountering legacy identity-linked result fragments after site removal.
findings.notes: This is distinct from live-source retrieval behavior.

findings.title: Ask Brave wrapper is not the indexing issue
findings.assessment: The AI-share wrapper page itself is not intended for search indexing.
findings.risk_implication: The relevant discovery issue lies in the underlying search or retrieval behavior rather than in the wrapper page itself.
findings.notes: The wrapper should not be treated as the primary indexing problem.

findings.title: Brave News query distortion

observed_condition.behavior: exact query normalized into an altered form

findings.assessment: This reflects loose query normalization, entity conflation, or both.
findings.risk_implication: Search behavior may produce irrelevant or misleading identity-adjacent results rather than true exact-match retrieval.
findings.notes: This should be tracked as a query-handling issue.

findings.title: Google behavior differs from Brave stale-result behavior

observed_condition.behavior: query-based retrieval of live public pages matching caption text or related source content

findings.assessment: The Google evidence reflects live-source retrieval behavior rather than the stale persistence pattern shown in Brave for a removed personal-site result.
findings.risk_implication: Google and Brave should not be treated as exhibiting the same discovery failure mode.
findings.notes: The distinction between stale removed-page residue and live public-source retrieval is material.

summary: The current technical record reflects multiple distinct discovery conditions, including a stale dead-route artifact, stale removed-site residue in Brave Search, a non-indexing Ask Brave wrapper surface, Brave query distortion, and a separate Google live-source retrieval pattern. These conditions should be classified separately rather than collapsed into a single search-discovery problem.
